title: wallet.sendTransaction called with skipPreflight: true skips simulation and silently lands failing/wrong txs

params.passDetail: sendTransaction runs preflight simulation (skipPreflight not set to true), so a transaction that would fail on-chain is rejected client-side before submission.
params.failDetail: sendTransaction is called with skipPreflight: true, disabling the preflight simulation. A malformed or failing transaction is submitted anyway; the happy path returns a signature but the transaction can fail or do the wrong thing on-chain with no client-side error.
params.absentCallDetail: No sendTransaction call found in this file.
params.absentArgDetail: sendTransaction is called without an options object, so skipPreflight defaults to false (preflight runs). Safe.

provenance.note: Agents copy skipPreflight: true from 'optimize transaction landing' guides to shave latency, not realizing it disables the client-side simulation that catches malformed/failing transactions. In the-hive this is the single send path used by every Privy embedded-wallet swap and transfer (hooks/privy/use-send-transaction.ts:97), so a failing tx is submitted with no error surfaced to the user.
